sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Emily Hernandez, a 20-year-old female, presents for her yearly follow-up for asthma, allergic rhinitis, and food allergy.
She has been using Wixela 250/50 dose, one puff in the morning and one puff at night, for approximately 3-4 days a week, with the remaining days taking one puff.
She uses her albuterol inhaler rarely, only for exertional activities.
She has a known peanut allergy, but tolerates almonds and cashews.
She has been using Zyrtec as needed, particularly during the spring and fall, for seasonal allergy symptoms.
She has a history of vaping and smoking, but has been trying to cut back.
She has an Epipen for emergencies.
Past medical history: Emily has a history of asthma, allergic rhinitis, and food allergy.
She was switched from Symbicort to Wixela due to insurance changes in April 2020.
She has been using Zyrtec for seasonal allergy symptoms.
Review of systems: Emily has not had any urgent care or ER visits for breathing issues in the past year.

Current medications: Wixela 250/50 dose, one puff in the morning and one puff at night, 3-4 days a week; albuterol inhaler as needed; Zyrtec as needed for seasonal allergy symptoms; Epipen for emergencies.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's ears looked good with some wax on both sides.
The doctor listened to her lungs and they sounded terrific.
The doctor also listened to her heart and noted that her breathing test results were similar to those from 2 years ago, but slightly better.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's asthma appears to be well-controlled, with minimal symptoms and no urgent care or ER visits in the past year.
Problem list: Asthma, active; Allergic rhinitis, active; Food allergy, active.
Plan:
I prescribed Alvesco 80 mcg, two puffs twice a day, to be used as a step-down therapy.
The patient will continue to use her albuterol inhaler as needed and Zyrtec as needed for seasonal allergy symptoms.
She will also continue to avoid peanuts and tree nuts.
I will refill her Epipen and albuterol inhaler.
Follow-up: The patient will return in 4 months for a repeat breathing test and to assess her response to the Alvesco therapy.
